_meta.description: SG-3 profile/register-keyed test & trap library (Phase 3e). Verbatim named tests and traps harvested from the 16 golden guides. Generation selects the chart's register bucket first, then falls back to 'any'. Consumed by tools/backfill_narratives.py and validated by SG-4's test/trap-presence check. Built by tools/build_test_trap_library.py from the golden guides.

quietLuxury: trust your gut and walk away

quietLuxury.failure: head-to-toe black reads like you gave up on the way out the door
quietLuxury.fix: break it up with one mid-tone layer from your base colours, or a scarf or belt in one of your accents

quietLuxury.failure: swaddling: layer upon layer of shapeless grey and the silhouette dissolves entirely
quietLuxury.fix: restore one defined line, a structured collar, a neat cuff, or let the keepsake pendant sit visibly at the neckline

quietLuxury.failure: dissolution: pale gauze on pale gauze with nothing beneath and the silhouette disappears
quietLuxury.fix: restore one line: a bias-cut slip that traces the body, a fitted base layer, or your relief colour worn closest to the skin

quietLuxury.failure: drowning in formless, head to toe black
quietLuxury.fix: break it with a single deliberate point of light, like a polished cuff or a comb that catches the lamplight

quietLuxury.failure: gilt overload: grandeur tips into gaudiness
quietLuxury.fix: hold the line at one molten flash and keep every other metal moment small, matt, and supporting

quietLuxury.failure: the all-earth sink: brown on olive on bark, all matt and all heavy, can swallow you whole
quietLuxury.fix: lift it: one pale layer at the collar, one piece of polished leather, or your relief colour

quietLuxury.failure: the sofa drift: soften every piece far enough and one day you are simply in loungewear
quietLuxury.fix: restore exactly one line: a rolled but real lapel, a trouser with a soft crease, or a firm leather bag against all that give

boldExpression.failure: the fade trap: washed-out pieces dilute your natural energy
boldExpression.fix: rescue the look immediately with a saturated accent at the neckline or a brilliant shoe

boldExpression.failure: a restrictive dark monochrome look that stifles your presence
boldExpression.fix: break the silhouette with one massive polished cuff or a shoe in your loudest accent

boldExpression.failure: costume: leather, studs, red lip, gold hardware and a smoky eye all at once tips drama into fancy dress
boldExpression.fix: hold the line at one molten flash and return every other element to matt black

versatileAdaptive.failure: the beige-out: every piece at the same pale value looks unfinished rather than minimal
versatileAdaptive.fix: rescue it with your darkest neutral as an anchor piece or one true accent, and let one texture change restore the depth

versatileAdaptive.failure: total ice: unbroken grey on grey can read as absence rather than composure
versatileAdaptive.fix: add the polished signal, a mirror-finish cuff, a patent boot, or one line of electric colour

versatileAdaptive.failure: a flat monochrome look that bores you by midday
versatileAdaptive.fix: add a crisp poplin collar, one architectural metal ring, or a bright geometric sneaker

versatileAdaptive.failure: prettiness without anchor: all-pale, all-soft reads charming then forgettable by lunch
versatileAdaptive.fix: give it one anchor: a sharply tailored blazer, a structured flat, or your calmest base colour worn where the eye lands first

versatileAdaptive.failure: the seesaw: one month all floaty gauze, the next all crisp utility
versatileAdaptive.fix: re-weave instead: keep one item from the other hand in every outfit, always

versatileAdaptive.failure: the hard shell: reaching for armour, technical jackets, and stiff utility layers when life speeds up
versatileAdaptive.fix: swap the shell for a brushed cotton layer that moves just as fast and holds you besides

quietLuxury: if a fabric resists your touch or feels squeaky between your fingers, leave it behind
boldExpression: the friction test (run your hand swiftly down the sleeve; seek the slip)

any: the pharmacy line: a trip to the pharmacy does not require suffering for the aesthetic
paying a utility bill does not require collapsing your silhouette
a midnight petrol station run still deserves a silhouette
a school run or a corner shop dash does not require surrendering your softness
a walk to the bakery does not require abandoning your atmosphere
a trip for milk does not require abandoning your silhouette
a hardware store run deserves the same clean fit as a Monday meeting
a trip to the dry cleaner does not suspend your standards
a market morning does not require abandoning proportion
walking the dog deserves the same standard as a meeting
a farmers' market run is still a small occasion
a rainy Sunday paper run deserves the same gentle standard
a packed day does not require hard clothes; it requires soft ones that can sprint
pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)
boldExpression: the reflection rule: hold the hardware under a direct light source; if it does not produce a sharp, blinding reflection, it is too dull

quietLuxury.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
quietLuxury.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

boldExpression.failure: oxidised, hammered, or antiqued finishes look tired and lack crispness
boldExpression.fix: stay strictly in polished metal that catches the light from across the street

versatileAdaptive.failure: antiqued brass, hammered copper, and artificially aged metal read as clutter against a present-tense style
versatileAdaptive.fix: keep to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome

any: would I still wear this in five to ten years (five-to-ten-year longevity)
boldExpression: the velocity of the garment: ask if you can walk fast in it
versatileAdaptive: the maintenance test: ask exactly what this piece needs from you every week
quietLuxury: the heirloom / record-time question: ask what the garment will look like after fifty wears, and buy only if the answer is better

quietLuxury.failure: buying things that only look good in photos
quietLuxury.fix: test the real hand-feel; a garment that is stiff or itchy in reality becomes an expensive paperweight

quietLuxury.failure: collecting cheap, seasonal trend pieces that lose their appeal by next year
quietLuxury.fix: invest slowly in pieces chosen with intention

versatileAdaptive.failure: trend pieces that need explaining; if it requires context, it fails

quietLuxury: remove the earring doing too much / one strong piece is enough
step back and soften focus (blur into texture)
the lamplight test (melt into a soft texture)
the underwater test (read like light moving on the seabed, not signage)
the twilight test (merge into a rich, deep texture)
the field test (settle into a natural texture like ground seen from a hill)
the firelight test (deepen and warm by lamplight)
the dusk test (settle into a warm, moving texture)
boldExpression: the ten foot rule (stay crisp, loud, and legible from across the room)
the twenty foot rule (bold and entirely legible from twenty feet)
the low-light rule (must still read as intent from ten feet in dim light)
versatileAdaptive: the desk test (read as clean texture at arm's length and a half)
the straight-edge test (lines stay crisp and true at three metres)
the blink test (not muddy or confusing in the first split second from ten feet)
the glance test (a pleasant impression rather than a specific shouting motif)
the arm's-length test (calm even texture far, rewarding weave close)
the passing test (reads as soft, pleasant texture in motion)

notes: boldExpression charts invert the legibility target: the distance test demands the pattern STAY high-contrast and legible from a distance (Ember ten-foot, Blaze twenty-foot, Cinder low-light), whereas quietLuxury and versatileAdaptive charts want the pattern to BLUR into texture at distance.
